title: Supply Chain — New Package Installs in CI Against Approved Registry

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST CSF GV.SC-01 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST CSF GV.SC-01 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.7.15: Supply Chain — New Package Installs in CI Against Approved Registry.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.7.15' running on github:audit or jenkins:console, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=cicd (sourcetype=github:audit OR sourcetype=jenkins:console) earliest=-7d "npm install" OR "pip install"
| rex field=_raw "(?i)(npm|pip)\s+install\s+(?<pkg>[^\s]+)"
| lookup approved_build_packages.csv pkg OUTPUT approved
| where isnull(approved)
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for the Change data model (Change.All_Changes).
description: Monitors CI pipelines for package installs that bypass internal registry mirrors, aligning with GV.SC supply chain risk management.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST CSF compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes cicd and sourcetypes github:audit, jenkins:console. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: We monitor CI pipelines for package installs that bypass internal registry mirrors, aligning with GV.SC supply chain risk management — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
implementation: (1) Seed approved internal registry mirror packages; (2) scope to release branches; (3) block on alert in pipeline optional; (4) quarterly supplier software review. Indexes required: `index=cicd`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=github:audit`, `sourcetype=jenkins:console`; Lookups: `approved_build_packages`, `approved_build_packages.csv`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: GitHub Enterprise audit or Jenkins.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `github:audit` or `jenkins:console`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Seed approved internal registry mirror packages; (2) scope to release branches; (3) block on alert in pipeline optional; (4) quarterly supplier software review.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cicd (sourcetype=github:audit OR sourcetype=jenkins:console) earliest=-7d "npm install" OR "pip install"
| rex field=_raw "(?i)(npm|pip)\s+install\s+(?<pkg>[^\s]+)"
| lookup approved_build_packages.csv pkg OUTPUT approved
| where isnull(approved)
```

Understanding this SPL

**Supply Chain — New Package Installs in CI Against Approved Registry (GV.SC-01)** — Monitors CI pipelines for package installs that bypass internal registry mirrors, aligning with GV.SC supply chain risk management.

Documented **Data sources**: `github:audit` or `jenkins:console`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): GitHub Enterprise audit or Jenkins. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cicd; **sourcetype**: github:audit, jenkins:console. Those sourcetypes align with what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cicd, sourcetype=github:audit, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Extracts fields with `rex` (regular expression).
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(approved)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Bar chart
knownFalsePositives: Identification gaps during new asset onboarding, M&A integration, or after refresh waves.
